claim: OpenAI Agents SDK provides Agent (assemble) and Runner (execute) classes for simpler multi-agent orchestration than Swarm
actionable: Use OpenAI Agents SDK to build agents: instantiate Agent with name/instructions/model, Runner to execute; supports tools, streaming, and agent handoffs
source_title: How to Build an Agent with the OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Agent instructions can inject context directly (e.g., menu prices) or pull dynamically via web search tool for real-time data
actionable: For static/fixed knowledge use direct injection; for dynamic/real-time knowledge use web search tools to avoid stale context
source_title: How to Build an Agent with the OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Web search tool retrieves annotations with source URLs that can be surfaced to users or scraped for richer RAG context
actionable: When using web search in agents, extract and store annotation URLs; optionally scrape them to build richer context for subsequent steps
source_title: How to Build an Agent with the OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Agents can be composed as tools for other agents, enabling multi-level orchestration and specialized agent delegation
actionable: Build specialized agents (e.g., McDonald's, In-N-Out pricing agents) and compose them as tools under an orchestrator agent (e.g., DoorDash comparison)
source_title: How to Build an Agent with the OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Persistent chat memory via input_list enables multi-turn conversations; trim old messages or summarize to stay within context limits
actionable: Use input_list to maintain conversation history between agent calls; implement trimming (last N messages) or summarization to control context size
source_title: How to Build an Agent with the OpenAI Agents SDK
